# Conditional Execution Test - Verify Environment-Driven Behavior
# Demonstrates: conditional logic, environment-based test variations, test branching

# Test execution with condition true
env FEATURE_ENABLED=true
exec sh -c 'if [ "$FEATURE_ENABLED" = "true" ]; then echo "Feature is enabled"; else exit 1; fi'
stdout "Feature is enabled"

# Test execution with condition false
env FEATURE_ENABLED=false
exec sh -c 'if [ "$FEATURE_ENABLED" = "false" ]; then echo "Feature is disabled"; else exit 1; fi'
stdout "Feature is disabled"

# Test skip logic
env SKIP_TEST=false
exec sh -c 'if [ "$SKIP_TEST" = "true" ]; then echo "Skipped"; exit 0; fi; echo "Executed"'
stdout "Executed"
! stdout "Skipped"

# Test with different values
env LEVEL=basic
exec sh -c 'case $LEVEL in basic) echo "Basic mode";; advanced) echo "Advanced";; esac'
stdout "Basic mode"

env LEVEL=advanced
exec sh -c 'case $LEVEL in basic) echo "Basic mode";; advanced) echo "Advanced";; esac'
stdout "Advanced"

# Test environment variable existence check
env CONFIG_PATH=/etc/config
exec sh -c 'if [ -n "$CONFIG_PATH" ]; then echo "Config path set"; fi'
stdout "Config path set"
